rulings.Case Title: EGNACIO E.R. BALINTON VS. BODYMASTERS SPORTS INDUSTRIES INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 24 Hour Fitness Usa, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 25, 2013, Line 21, Defendant 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish causes of action 1-3 (products liability). The undisputed evidence demonstrates that defendant is in the business of providing fitness services and the squat machine was an incident to those services. See Ontiveros v. 24 Hour Fitness (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 424. The doctrine of primary assumption of the risk bars causes of action 4 and 5. Defendants owed no legal duty to eliminate (or protect a plaintiff against) risks inherent in the activity. Defendant did nothing to increase the danger and there is no evidence of gross negligence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: GENERAL WORKING INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MOTI KAHANA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 25, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT TODD DAUM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Daum's demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is sustained with leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action, and overruled as to the fourth and sixth causes of action. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant's demurrer to the second and third causes of action is sustained. Plaintiff has failed to plead facts with sufficient specificity regarding Defendant Daum's alleged misrepresentation. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to plead more facts regarding Defendant Daum's alleged misrepresentation. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for services rendered is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts that it performed IT services for the direct benefit of Defendant, and that Defendant did not pay the reasonable value of services, $75,000. (FAC 33-37.) Defendant's demurrer to the sixth cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts that Defendant knew or had reason to know that the alleged trade secrets were acquired by improper means. (FAC 46.) Whether the common law claims are preempted by Civ. Code § 3426 et seq is a factual question. (See KC Mulitimedia, Inc. v. Bank of America Technology & Operations, Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 939, 954.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: NANTONG JOYFUL FASHION CO., LTD et al VS. GEORGIOU STUDIO, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Nantong Joyful Fashion Co., Ltd'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 25, 2013, Line 22, Plaintiff Nantong Joyful Fashion Co., Ltd's Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Plaintiffs' Application for Right to Attach Order and Order for Issuance of Writ of Attachment is granted as to Defendants Georgiou Studio, Inc. and George Georgiou. Per §484.090, the Court finds that (1) the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued, (2) Plaintiffs have established the probable validity of the claim upon which the attachment is based, (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based, and (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. Plaintiffs shall post an undertaking in the amount of $10,000 pursuant to CCP §489.020. Defendants' objection to the amount of undertaking is overruled. (See North Hollywood Marble Co. v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 683, 690-91, holding a plaintiff should not be required to post a "massive undertaking whenever the defendant can demonstrate that it is in such a precarious financial condition that a lawful attachment would result in probable damage or destruction of its entire business.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: MAX MARMER VS. BJOERN LASSE HERRMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 25, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTS BJOERN HERRMANN, ERTAN DOGRULTAN, STARTUP COMPASS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained in part. Overruled as to the first cause of action, Plaintiff has properly alleged that Defendants used their majority of shares to injury Plaintiff. Overruled as to the second cause of action, Plaintiff has properly alleged a right to possess net profits from the corporation. Overruled as to the third cause of action, Plaintiff pled facts with sufficient particularity. Sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege what monies or property Defendants obtained from Plaintiff through the use of an unfair business practice. Overruled as to the fifth cause of action, demurrer serves no useful purpose. Sustained with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a prospective controversy that will not be resolved by another cause of action. Overruled as to the seventh cause of action, the demurrer serves no useful purpose. Overruled as to the ninth cause of action, the demurrer serves no useful purpose. Sustained with leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege the cause of action against his employer. Overruled as to the fourteenth cause of action, the demurrer serves no useful purpose. Overruled as to the fifteenth cause of action, Plaintiff has properly alleged that Defendants used their majority of shares to injury Plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)
